metadata.title:
THIRD WARD
WILL LEAD


IN PAVING

pages.raw_text:
President E. V. Lambert of the
city council announced this morn
ing that the plans for paving the
Cannon Hill district will be pre-
sented to the city council next
Tuesday night.


The plans are still in the hands
of the city engineer, who has the
work about ready for the inspec-
ton of the city council. The area
of the improvement is about one
square mile and covers every street
in the district between Fourth ave-
nue. on the north and Tenth avenue
on the south, Monroe street on the
east and Hangman creek on the
west. The character of the paving
to be used is asphalt on the level
streets and brick and concrete on
the grades. The estimated cost is
100000.


With the combined Brownies add
ition and Cannon Hill paving COM
pleted the Third Ward will have
taken a stride ahead of any other
section of the city in the matter of
paving. These two jobs will joint
ly represent about $1,000,000, to be
invested in street paving in that
section of the city within the pres
ent year.
